speaker: MORTIMER
text: These promises are fair, the parties sure,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And our induction full of prosperous hope.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Lord Mortimer and cousin Glendower,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Will you sit down? And uncle Worcester—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A plague upon it, I have forgot the map.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: No, here it is. Sit, cousin Percy,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Sit, good cousin Hotspur, for by that name

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: As oft as Lancaster doth speak of you

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: His cheek looks pale, and with a rising sigh

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: He wisheth you in heaven.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And you in hell,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: As oft as he hears Owen Glendower spoke of.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: I cannot blame him. At my nativity

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: The front of heaven was full of fiery shapes,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Of burning cressets, and at my birth

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: The frame and huge foundation of the Earth

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Shaked like a coward.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Why, so it would have done

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: At the same season if your mother’s cat

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Had but kittened, though yourself had never been born.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: I say the Earth did shake when I was born.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And I say the Earth was not of my mind,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: If you suppose as fearing you it shook.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: The heavens were all on fire; the Earth did tremble.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: O, then the Earth shook to see the heavens on fire,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And not in fear of your nativity.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Diseasèd nature oftentimes breaks forth

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In strange eruptions; oft the teeming Earth

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Is with a kind of colic pinched and vexed

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: By the imprisoning of unruly wind

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Within her womb, which, for enlargement striving,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Shakes the old beldam Earth and topples down

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Steeples and moss-grown towers. At your birth

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Our grandam Earth, having this distemp’rature,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In passion shook.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Cousin, of many men

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: I do not bear these crossings. Give me leave

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: To tell you once again that at my birth

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: The front of heaven was full of fiery shapes,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: The goats ran from the mountains, and the herds

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Were strangely clamorous to the frighted fields.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: These signs have marked me extraordinary,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: And all the courses of my life do show

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: I am not in the roll of common men.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Where is he living, clipped in with the sea

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: That chides the banks of England, Scotland, Wales,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Which calls me pupil or hath read to me?

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: And bring him out that is but woman’s son

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Can trace me in the tedious ways of art

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: And hold me pace in deep experiments.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I think there’s no man speaks better Welsh.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I’ll to dinner.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Peace, cousin Percy. You will make him mad.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: I can call spirits from the vasty deep.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Why, so can I, or so can any man,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But will they come when you do call for them?

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Why, I can teach you, cousin, to command the devil.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And I can teach thee, coz, to shame the devil

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: By telling truth. Tell truth and shame the devil.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: If thou have power to raise him, bring him hither,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And I’ll be sworn I have power to shame him hence.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: O, while you live, tell truth and shame the devil!

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Come, come, no more of this unprofitable chat.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Three times hath Henry Bolingbroke made head

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Against my power; thrice from the banks of Wye

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: And sandy-bottomed Severn have I sent him

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Bootless home and weather-beaten back.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Home without boots, and in foul weather too!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: How ’scapes he agues, in the devil’s name?

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Come, here is the map. Shall we divide our right

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: According to our threefold order ta’en?

speaker: MORTIMER
text: The Archdeacon hath divided it

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Into three limits very equally:

speaker: MORTIMER
text: England, from Trent and Severn hitherto,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: By south and east is to my part assigned;

speaker: MORTIMER
text: All westward, Wales beyond the Severn shore,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And all the fertile land within that bound

speaker: MORTIMER
text: To Owen Glendower; and, dear coz, to you

speaker: MORTIMER
text: The remnant northward lying off from Trent.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And our indentures tripartite are drawn,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Which being sealèd interchangeably—

speaker: MORTIMER
text: A business that this night may execute—

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Tomorrow, cousin Percy, you and I

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And my good Lord of Worcester will set forth

speaker: MORTIMER
text: To meet your father and the Scottish power,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: As is appointed us, at Shrewsbury.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: My father Glendower is not ready yet,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Nor shall we need his help these fourteen days.

speaker: MORTIMER
text:
To
                 
                Glendower
                .
                 
              Within that space you may have drawn together

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Your tenants, friends, and neighboring gentlemen.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: A shorter time shall send me to you, lords,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: And in my conduct shall your ladies come,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: From whom you now must steal and take no leave,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: For there will be a world of water shed

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Upon the parting of your wives and you.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Methinks my moiety, north from Burton here,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In quantity equals not one of yours.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: See how this river comes me cranking in

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And cuts me from the best of all my land

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A huge half-moon, a monstrous cantle out.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I’ll have the current in this place dammed up,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And here the smug and silver Trent shall run

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In a new channel, fair and evenly.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: It shall not wind with such a deep indent

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To rob me of so rich a bottom here.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Not wind? It shall, it must. You see it doth.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Yea, but mark how he bears his course, and runs me up

speaker: MORTIMER
text: With like advantage on the other side,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Gelding the opposèd continent as much

speaker: MORTIMER
text: As on the other side it takes from you.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Yea, but a little charge will trench him here

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And on this north side win this cape of land,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And then he runs straight and even.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I’ll have it so. A little charge will do it.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: I’ll not have it altered.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Will not you?

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: No, nor you shall not.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Who shall say me nay?

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Why, that will I.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Let me not understand you, then; speak it in Welsh.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: I can speak English, lord, as well as you,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: For I was trained up in the English court,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Where being but young I framèd to the harp

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Many an English ditty lovely well

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: And gave the tongue a helpful ornament—

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: A virtue that was never seen in you.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Marry, and I am glad of it with all my heart.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I had rather be a kitten and cry “mew”

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Than one of these same meter balladmongers.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I had rather hear a brazen can’stick turned,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Or a dry wheel grate on the axletree,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And that would set my teeth nothing an edge,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Nothing so much as mincing poetry.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: ’Tis like the forced gait of a shuffling nag.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Come, you shall have Trent turned.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I do not care. I’ll give thrice so much land

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To any well-deserving friend;

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But in the way of bargain, mark you me,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I’ll cavil on the ninth part of a hair.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Are the indentures drawn? Shall we be gone?

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: The moon shines fair. You may away by night.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: I’ll haste the writer, and withal

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Break with your wives of your departure hence.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: I am afraid my daughter will run mad,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: So much she doteth on her Mortimer.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Fie, cousin Percy, how you cross my father!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I cannot choose. Sometime he angers me

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: With telling me of the moldwarp and the ant,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Of the dreamer Merlin and his prophecies,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And of a dragon and a finless fish,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A clip-winged griffin and a moulten raven,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A couching lion and a ramping cat,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And such a deal of skimble-skamble stuff

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: As puts me from my faith. I tell you what—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: He held me last night at least nine hours

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In reckoning up the several devils’ names

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: That were his lackeys. I cried “Hum,” and “Well, go to,”

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: But marked him not a word. O, he is as tedious

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: As a tired horse, a railing wife,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Worse than a smoky house. I had rather live

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: With cheese and garlic in a windmill, far,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Than feed on cates and have him talk to me

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: In any summer house in Christendom.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: In faith, he is a worthy gentleman,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Exceedingly well read and profited

speaker: MORTIMER
text: In strange concealments, valiant as a lion,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And wondrous affable, and as bountiful

speaker: MORTIMER
text: As mines of India. Shall I tell you, cousin?

speaker: MORTIMER
text: He holds your temper in a high respect

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And curbs himself even of his natural scope

speaker: MORTIMER
text: When you come cross his humor. Faith, he does.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: I warrant you that man is not alive

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Might so have tempted him as you have done

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Without the taste of danger and reproof.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: But do not use it oft, let me entreat you.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: In faith, my lord, you are too willful-blame,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And, since your coming hither, have done enough

speaker: WORCESTER
text: To put him quite besides his patience.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: You must needs learn, lord, to amend this fault.

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Though sometimes it show greatness, courage, blood—

speaker: WORCESTER
text: And that’s the dearest grace it renders you—

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Yet oftentimes it doth present harsh rage,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Defect of manners, want of government,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Pride, haughtiness, opinion, and disdain,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: The least of which, haunting a nobleman,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Loseth men’s hearts and leaves behind a stain

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Upon the beauty of all parts besides,

speaker: WORCESTER
text: Beguiling them of commendation.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Well, I am schooled. Good manners be your speed!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Here come our wives, and let us take our leave.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: This is the deadly spite that angers me:

speaker: MORTIMER
text: My wife can speak no English, I no Welsh.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: My daughter weeps; she’ll not part with you.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: She’ll be a soldier too, she’ll to the wars.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Good father, tell her that she and my aunt Percy

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Shall follow in your conduct speedily.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: She is desperate here, a peevish self-willed harlotry,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: One that no persuasion can do good upon.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: I understand thy looks. That pretty Welsh

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Which thou pourest down from these swelling heavens

speaker: MORTIMER
text: I am too perfect in, and but for shame

speaker: MORTIMER
text: In such a parley should I answer thee.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: I understand thy kisses, and thou mine,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: And that’s a feeling disputation;

speaker: MORTIMER
text: But I will never be a truant, love,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Till I have learned thy language; for thy tongue

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Makes Welsh as sweet as ditties highly penned,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: Sung by a fair queen in a summer’s bower,

speaker: MORTIMER
text: With ravishing division, to her lute.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Nay, if you melt, then will she run mad.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: O, I am ignorance itself in this!

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: She bids you on the wanton rushes lay you down

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: And rest your gentle head upon her lap,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: And she will sing the song that pleaseth you,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: And on your eyelids crown the god of sleep,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Charming your blood with pleasing heaviness,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Making such difference ’twixt wake and sleep

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: As is the difference betwixt day and night

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: The hour before the heavenly harnessed team

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Begins his golden progress in the east.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: With all my heart I’ll sit and hear her sing.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: By that time will our book, I think, be drawn.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Do so, and those musicians that shall play to you

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Hang in the air a thousand leagues from hence,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: And straight they shall be here. Sit and attend.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Come, Kate, thou art perfect in lying down.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Come, quick, quick, that I may lay my head in thy lap.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Go, you giddy goose.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Now I perceive the devil understands Welsh,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And ’tis no marvel he is so humorous.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: By ’r Lady, he is a good musician.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Then should you be nothing but musical,

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: for you are altogether governed by humors. Lie

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: still, you thief, and hear the lady sing in Welsh.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: I had rather hear Lady, my brach, howl in

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Irish.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Wouldst thou have thy head broken?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: No.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Then be still.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Neither; ’tis a woman’s fault.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Now God help thee!

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To the Welsh lady’s bed.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: What’s that?

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Peace, she sings.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Come, Kate, I’ll have your song too.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: Not mine, in good sooth.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Not yours, in good sooth! Heart, you swear

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: like a comfit-maker’s wife! “Not you, in good

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: sooth,” and “as true as I live,” and “as God shall

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: mend me,” and “as sure as day”—

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And givest such sarcenet surety for thy oaths

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: As if thou never walk’st further than Finsbury.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Swear me, Kate, like a lady as thou art,

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: A good mouth-filling oath, and leave “in sooth,”

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: And such protest of pepper-gingerbread

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: To velvet-guards and Sunday citizens.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: Come, sing.

speaker:
LADY
               
              PERCY
text: I will not sing.

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: ’Tis the next way to turn tailor, or be redbreast

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: teacher. An the indentures be drawn, I’ll

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: away within these two hours, and so come in when

speaker: HOTSPUR
text: you will.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: Come, come, Lord Mortimer, you are as slow

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: As hot Lord Percy is on fire to go.

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: By this our book is drawn. We’ll but seal,

speaker: GLENDOWER
text: And then to horse immediately.

speaker: MORTIMER
text: With all my heart.
